title: Piece by Piece Fun Day
story: Every January 29, puzzle lovers everywhere celebrate National Puzzle Day by breaking out their favorite brain teasers! People gather around kitchen tables to work on massive jigsaw puzzles, challenge friends to crossword competitions, and solve tricky riddles together. Libraries host puzzle swaps where families trade their completed puzzles for new ones. Schools organize puzzle tournaments with everything from word searches to Rubik's cubes. Social media fills with photos of people proudly showing off their finished masterpieces. It's the perfect day to exercise your brain and discover just how satisfying it feels when that final puzzle piece clicks into place!
activity_question: What do libraries often organize on National Puzzle Day?
activity_choices: Puzzle swaps where people trade completed puzzles
Free pizza parties for puzzle solvers
activity_answer: Puzzle swaps where people trade completed puzzles
follow_up_question: What's the most challenging puzzle you've ever completed?

title: Crunch Time for Corn Chips!
story: Every January 29, corn chip lovers across America celebrate National Corn Chip Day by munching their way through bags of crunchy goodness! People host chip-tasting parties, trying different brands and flavors from spicy nacho cheese to cool ranch. Restaurants offer special deals on nachos and loaded corn chip platters. Social media fills with photos of creative corn chip recipes like walking tacos, chip-crusted chicken, and even corn chip cookies. Some families have contests to see who can crunch the loudest or build the tallest chip tower without it falling over!
activity_question: What do some families do with corn chips on this holiday?
activity_choices: Have contests to see who can crunch the loudest
activity_answer: Have contests to see who can crunch the loudest
bonus_fact: Americans eat over 4 billion pounds of corn chips every year!

title: Carnation Celebration Day
story: Pink, red, and white carnations take center stage every January 29 on National Carnation Day! People buy these ruffled flowers to brighten their homes, give bouquets to friends, or wear them as special pins. Carnations last longer than most flowers, making them perfect for celebrating friendship and love that lasts.
activity_question: What makes carnations especially good for celebrating friendship?
activity_choices: They last longer than most flowers
activity_answer: They last longer than most flowers
bonus_fact: Carnations are the official flower for Mother's Day in many countries!

title: Think Outside the Box Day!
story: Every January 29, people celebrate Freethinkers Day by questioning everything and thinking for themselves! This special day encourages everyone to ask "why" and "what if" instead of just accepting what they're told. Kids might debate topics at school, families discuss different viewpoints at dinner, or friends share wild new ideas. It's all about using your brain to form your own opinions and being brave enough to think differently from the crowd.
activity_question: What does Freethinkers Day encourage people to do?
activity_choices: Think for themselves and question things
Give away free thoughts to strangers
Stop thinking for the whole day
activity_answer: Think for themselves and question things
follow_up_question: What's one thing you've always wondered about but never questioned before?

title: Four-Legged Heroes Get Their Day
story: Every January 29, people celebrate National Seeing Eye Dog Day to honor the amazing dogs that help blind and visually impaired people navigate the world safely. These specially trained dogs learn to stop at curbs, avoid obstacles, and guide their handlers around dangers like low-hanging branches or construction zones. On this day, people share photos and stories about these incredible working dogs on social media, donate to guide dog organizations, and learn about the months of training these pups go through. Many schools invite guide dog trainers to visit and demonstrate how these furry heroes work, showing students the special harnesses and commands used daily.
activity_question: What do seeing eye dogs help their handlers avoid?
activity_choices: Obstacles and dangers like curbs and low branches
activity_answer: Obstacles and dangers like curbs and low branches
follow_up_question: What other jobs do you think specially trained dogs could help people with?

title: Pack Your Bags for Planning Day!
story: Every January 29, people across America celebrate National Plan for Vacation Day by mapping out their dream getaways for the year ahead. Families gather around computers and travel books, researching destinations, comparing prices, and marking calendars with potential trip dates. Some people create vision boards with photos of beaches, mountains, or theme parks they want to visit. Others use this day to finally book that vacation they've been talking about all winter!
activity_question: What do families often use to research vacation destinations on this holiday?
follow_up_question: If you could plan any vacation, where would you want to go and why?

title: Grumpy and Proud of It Day
story: Every grumpy person gets their moment to shine on January 29 during Curmudgeons Day! A curmudgeon is someone who complains a lot and finds fault with everything around them. On this silly holiday, people celebrate their inner grump by posting cranky memes online, wearing frowny face shirts, or playfully complaining about everything from weather to homework. Some families even have "grumpy contests" to see who can make the best annoyed face or come up with the funniest complaint about everyday things.
activity_choices: Someone who complains a lot and finds fault with things
A person who collects old coins
activity_answer: Someone who complains a lot and finds fault with things
bonus_fact: The word "curmudgeon" has been used for over 400 years to describe grouchy people!

title: Silly Messages Make January 29 Fun!
story: Your voicemail greeting is probably pretty boring, but on January 29, National Inane Answering Message Day changes everything! People record the silliest, weirdest, and most ridiculous voicemail messages they can think of. Some pretend to be robots, others sing opera, and many add funny sound effects or joke like they're famous celebrities. It's a day to be creative and make anyone who calls you laugh out loud instead of hearing the same old "leave a message" routine.
activity_question: What do people do with their voicemail messages on this holiday?
follow_up_question: What would be the funniest voicemail greeting you could create?

title: Backwards Day: Everything's Topsy-Turvy!
story: On January 29, people celebrate National Backwards Day by doing everything in reverse! Kids wear their clothes backwards, walk backwards to school, and say "goodbye" when they arrive. Some families eat dessert first, then dinner. Teachers might start class by saying "see you later" instead of "hello." People write their names backwards and try talking in reverse sentences. Social media fills with upside-down photos and backwards videos. It's a silly day that reminds us to look at the world differently and have fun breaking normal rules. The best part? Everything wrong becomes right on this topsy-turvy holiday!
activity_question: What do people typically do with their clothes on National Backwards Day?
follow_up_question: What would be the most challenging thing to do backwards for a whole day?

title: Stack Attack: National Blueberry Pancake Day
story: Fluffy pancakes loaded with juicy blueberries take center stage every January 29 during National Blueberry Pancake Day! Families across America fire up their griddles and whip up towering stacks of these purple-spotted breakfast treats. Restaurants offer special blueberry pancake deals, while home cooks experiment with wild recipes like blueberry pancake muffins, pancake art, and even blueberry pancake ice cream. Social media fills with drool-worthy photos of syrup cascading down berry-packed stacks. Some people host pancake parties where everyone brings different toppings like whipped cream, chocolate chips, or extra berries. It's the perfect excuse to eat dessert for breakfast!
activity_question: What do people often share on social media during National Blueberry Pancake Day?
activity_choices: Pictures of their pets eating pancakes
bonus_fact: Maine produces about 99% of America's wild blueberries!
